home.mainTitle: 2EASY is an interactive platform with ready-made materials for creating and teaching language lessons
home.createLessonsTitle: Create lessons with our simple, intuitive builder
home.createLessonsDesc: Interactive tasks, texts, videos, and much more - all in the easy-to-use 2EASY builder
home.lessonPlansDesc: Our ready-to-teach lessons are the pride of 2EASY. Authentic materials are carefully adapted for different student levels.
home.studentAccountsDesc: Add your students and teach in real time
home.lessonPlansSectionDesc: Ready-made lessons on relevant topics with juicy vocabulary, authentic videos and articles, complemented by grammar, exercises and quizzes
home.speakingGamesSectionDesc: Games that work great for warm up and any part of the lesson: they really warm up before class and boost the mood
home.discussionCardsSectionDesc: Non-trivial questions on relevant topics: psychology, relationships, business, feminism and more

grammarPage.title: GRAMMAR
grammarPage.subtitle: No theory, rules, or fill-in-the-blank drills here. We’ve collected only speaking-focused exercises and games, so after learning a grammar topic, your student can immediately practice it in speech.

subscription.title: 2easy Subscription

page.subtitle: Access to materials and the lesson builder platform
page.description1: A subscription gives you access to materials and updates released during your subscription period, the lesson builder platform, and your student dashboard for teaching in real time.
page.description2: Your subscription renews automatically. You can cancel anytime in your account or via

subscription.description: Access to materials, lesson creation platform and student dashboard for conducting real-time lessons
subscription.tariffIncludes: Each plan includes full access to everything on the site:
subscription.constructorAccess: constructor for creating interactive lessons and courses
subscription.allGames: all speaking games (Taboo, Would you rather, etc) and discussion cards (150+)
subscription.freeTrialDesc: You'll have 7 days of access to the lesson constructor and part of the materials -- this will help you get acquainted with the platform before subscribing.

focusScroll.tooltip: Use this if students got lost during the lesson. When you click the button, all students in the lesson will be moved to the current task you’re looking at.
focusScroll.cantDetectCurrentTask: Couldn't detect the current task on the screen

editor.confirmDeleteEx: Are you sure you want to delete this task?
editor.fillGapsHintSelect: Select the word in the text you want to skip. Click the arrow to add other options.
editor.fillGapsHintDrag: Select the word in the text you want to skip. The list of skipped words will automatically appear above the text.
editor.searchPlaceholder: What are we looking for? (e.g.: cats)

templates.textDesc: Articles, questions and other text blocks
templates.fillGapsDesc: Drag, type or select the correct word
templates.testDesc: Select the correct option from the given choices
templates.matchDesc: Match word with picture or definition
templates.integrationsDesc: Miro, Google Drive, Wordwall, Genially, Quizlet, etc

404.title: This page doesn't exist.
404.moreInteresting: – there's lots of interesting stuff there

taboo.title: Taboo game
taboo.subTitle: 90 cards, A2 -- B2 + slang
taboo.description: exciting game where players must explain a word without using the list of «taboo words» on the card

neverHaveIEver.title: Never have I ever
neverHaveIEver.subTitle: 130+ cards, A2 -- B2
neverHaveIEver.description: perfect game for practicing Present Perfect. 80 cards with statements that are easy to use in the last 5 minutes of class or include as a full activity

wouldYouRather.title: Would you rather
wouldYouRather.subTitle: 50+ cards, A2+
wouldYouRather.description: Cards with thought-provoking questions where the student needs to choose one of the suggested options, e.g.: «Would you rather: be single for 5 years or not travel for 5 years?»

whatHappensNext.title: What happens next
whatHappensNext.subTitle: 30 short videos, A2 -- B2
whatHappensNext.description: Game aimed at practicing the «be going to» structure for A2 level students and above. students must guess what happens next by watching a short video

whatHappensNextFilms.title: What happens next
whatHappensNextFilms.subTitle: Films & series
whatHappensNextFilms.description: students watch a short video clip from a film/series and try to guess what happens next using the «be going to» structure

ifYouCould.title: If you could
ifYouCould.subTitle: 30 cards, A2+
ifYouCould.description: students take turns asking each other questions with «IF you could», e.g.: «If you could travel anywhere in the world, where would you go?»

nameThree.title: Name three
nameThree.subTitle: 90 cards, A2+
nameThree.description: game where students need to name 3 specific things in 5-10-15 seconds (as the teacher decides 💅🏼). Great option when there are a few minutes left until the end of the lesson that need to be spent productively

discussionCards.title: Discussion cards
discussionCards.subTitle: 140+ cards, A2+
discussionCards.description: Over 140 non-trivial questions on various life topics — from food to feminism and business. Suitable for A2 level students and above

controversial.title: Controversial statements
controversial.subTitle: 30 cards, A2+
controversial.description: cards with controversial statements on various topics, helping the student to conduct a discussion in English

firstMeeting.title: First meeting
firstMeeting.subTitle: 30 cards, A2+
firstMeeting.description: cards with non-trivial questions for getting to know a new student

subscriptionCancel.shareWhyTitle: Share why you decided to cancel your subscription
subscriptionCancel.placeholderOther: Would you like to tell us more?
subscriptionCancel.reasonOtherPlatforms: I use other platforms for work
subscriptionCancel.reasonInconvenient: inconvenient to conduct lessons on the platform
subscriptionCancel.reasonQuality: don't like the quality of materials
